item.title: Manila tricycle drivers share struggles amid high fuel prices; federation eyes new fare hike petition
item.description: Hundreds of tricycle drivers in Manila trooped to designated payout centers to receive ₱5,000 in cash relief assistance distributed by the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD), as part of the government’s response to the surge in fuel prices driven by the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: EU eyes deeper ties with Philippines amid global challenges, says Ambassador Santoro
item.description: EU Ambassador to the Philippines Massimo Santoro paid his first visit to a local broadcast media network on Monday, sitting down with ABS-CBN for an interview that touched on the ripple effects of the US-Iran war, EU security cooperation with Manila, and the status of free trade negotiations.

item.title: Emerging modus operandi? Imee flags ‘rampant practice’ of appointing acting Cabinet secretaries
item.description: Sen. Imee Marcos noted that temporary designations should not extend indefinitely, but should only be from six months to a year.

item.title: 7 minors rescued over alleged school attack plot
item.description: Authorities have rescued seven minors after intelligence information revealed alleged online discussions about staging a school attack, the Philippine National Police Anti-Cybercrime Group (PNP-ACG) said.

item.title: DOJ prosecutors approve additional respondents in Revilla plunder case
item.description: The panel of prosecutors of the Department of Justice has approved the addition of four more respondents in the plunder case of former senator Ramon "Bong" Revilla, Jr. in connection with allegedly anomalous flood control projects.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
